metadata.domain: Required and Recommended Training Topics by Area

general.description: Topics applicable to all plant areas and all employees

topics.content_summary: Energy isolation procedures, authorized vs affected employees, group LOTO, verification of zero energy state, lock removal protocols
Permit-required confined space identification, atmospheric testing (O2, LEL, CO, H2S), ventilation requirements, attendant duties, rescue plan, entry/exit procedures
Fall hazard identification, guardrail systems, personal fall arrest systems, harness inspection, anchor point selection, travel restraint vs fall arrest, rescue planning
applicable_roles: all personnel working at heights above 6 feet

topics.content_summary: Fire triangle, hot work permits, fire extinguisher types and use (PASS method), flammable material storage, conveyor belt fire prevention, kiln area fire hazards
Basic first aid, CPR/AED use, bleeding control, shock treatment, burn treatment, heat illness response, when to call emergency services
applicable_roles: minimum one certified person per shift

topics.content_summary: Alarm types and meanings, evacuation routes by area, rally points, headcount procedures, shelter-in-place vs evacuation criteria, communication chain

quarry.description: Topics specific to quarry, crushing, and raw material extraction operations

topics.content_summary: Highwall inspection before each shift, bench stability assessment, safe distances from edges, loose material hazards, scaling procedures, restricted zones during blasting
Pre-shift inspection, seatbelt use, berms and dump points, haul road rules, blind spot awareness, backing procedures, load securement, equipment-specific controls

topics.frequency: Initial + annual refresher (blast crew: specialized certification)
topics.content_summary: Blast zone clearance procedures, guard posting, warning signals and timing, misfires, flyrock protection, blast area re-entry, pre-blast and post-blast inspections

Respirable dust sources and silica content, dust suppression systems, personal dust monitoring, noise exposure limits (90 dBA TWA), hearing conservation program, audiometric testing, proper use of hearing protection

crushing_screening.description: Topics specific to primary, secondary, and tertiary crushing and screening operations

topics.content_summary: Identification of nip points on crushers, screens, and conveyors; proper guarding verification; body positioning; never reach into operating equipment; LOTO before clearing
Guard types (fixed, interlocked, adjustable), guard inspection, reporting missing or damaged guards, guard removal only under LOTO, guard replacement before restart
LOTO before entry, approved tools and methods, bridging hazards in hoppers, never enter crusher cavity without full isolation, communication with control room, spotter requirements
Water spray systems at transfer points, bag filter operation and maintenance, enclosure integrity checks, personal dust monitoring, respiratory protection when systems are down

grinding.description: Topics specific to raw mill and finish mill (cement mill) grinding operations

topics.content_summary: Full LOTO of mill drive, auxiliary drive, and feed systems; atmospheric testing; ventilation; body harness for vertical mills; communication protocol; hot surface cooling time; grinding media shifting hazards
Charging media safely, mechanical aids for heavy balls/cylpebs, overhead crane use during charging, exclusion zone during media addition, noise protection, pinch points at mill inlet
Surface temperatures on mills, separators, and ducts can exceed 300F; burn prevention; required PPE (heat-resistant gloves); cooling time after shutdown; hot surface signage

kiln_area.description: Topics specific to preheater, calciner, rotary kiln, and clinker cooler operations

topics.topic: Hot Surface and Thermal Burn Prevention
topics.content_summary: Kiln shell temperatures up to 700F, preheater tower surfaces, cooler grate areas; required approach distances; heat-resistant PPE; no exposed skin policy in kiln area; burn treatment procedures
Kiln rotation hazards, tire and roller pinch points, thrust roller area restrictions, drive guard integrity, kiln turning gear procedures, no loose clothing policy

Carbon monoxide generation during kiln upset, SO2 from raw material sulfur content, fixed gas monitors and alarm levels (CO: 50 ppm TWA, SO2: 5 ppm TWA), portable gas detector use, evacuation triggers, preheater tower atmospheric hazards

topics.frequency: Task training before each shutdown campaign
topics.content_summary: Brick and castable hazards, silica dust from refractory demolition, fall protection on scaffolding inside kiln, LOTO of kiln drive and auxiliary, heat exposure limits, hydration burns from castable, chrome-containing refractory handling

material_handling.description: Topics specific to conveyors, silos, hoppers, and bulk material transport

topics.content_summary: Nip points at head, tail, and bend pulleys; belt tracking hazards; emergency pull cord inspection and testing; walkway clearances; no riding on conveyors; startup warning procedures; crossing conveyors only at designated crossovers

Never enter a silo with material unless full confined space and engulfment protections are in place; bridging and rat-holing hazards; vibrator and air cannon safe use; silo level monitoring; no standing on material surface
Belt friction as ignition source, belt slip detection, hot material on belt, fire suppression systems at transfer points, belt fire rated specifications, housekeeping around conveyor galleries

electrical.description: Topics specific to electrical work and electrical safety in plant operations

topics.content_summary: Arc flash hazard analysis labels, approach boundaries (flash protection, limited, restricted, prohibited), required arc-rated PPE by incident energy level, de-energize before work policy, emergency response to arc flash injuries
Electrical-specific LOTO steps, verifying zero voltage with rated meter, stored energy (capacitors), multiple feed isolation, temporary grounding for high voltage, permit system for energized work

Definition of qualified person for electrical work, work restrictions for unqualified persons, approach distances by voltage level, when to call a qualified electrician, reporting electrical hazards (exposed wires, damaged cords, missing covers)

packing_shipping.description: Topics specific to cement packing, palletizing, loading, and shipping operations

topics.content_summary: Truck routing and speed limits in loading area, pedestrian exclusion zones during loading, spotter use for backing, wheel chocking during loading, driver must remain in cab or designated safe area, high-visibility vest requirement

Proper lifting techniques, 50-lb single-person lift limit guideline, mechanical aids available, bag stacking height limits, repetitive motion injury prevention, stretching program, reporting musculoskeletal symptoms early

Cement dust as respiratory and skin irritant, hexavalent chromium in some cements, dust collection system operation, bag filling station ventilation, respiratory protection selection, dermatitis prevention (gloves, barrier cream, washing)
